commands.review_summary:
The PR changes doctor runtime tool schema collection to catch agent and bundled MCP tool load, normalization, and pre-normalization failures and return `core/doctor/runtime-tool-schemas` health findings with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +111, Tests +168. Total +279 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can let `createOpenClawCodingTools` or `normalizeAgentRuntimeTools` throw from doctor runtime schema collection, and the PR adds focused tests for those paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed because this protected PR is stacked on an open base and changes doctor error-level behavior; there is no narrow automated repair target.

target.title: fix(doctor): report runtime tool schema errors
target.body:
## Summary

- reports agent runtime tool construction failures as `core/doctor/runtime-tool-schemas` findings instead of aborting doctor
- reports RuntimePlan/provider schema normalization failures for agent and bundled MCP tools without aborting the rest of runtime schema validation
- records pre-normalization unreadable tool-schema diagnostics through the same runtime normalization path used by assistant turns

This is stacked on #88735 and intentionally contains only the doctor/reporting follow-up.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/runtime-tool-schema-quarantine-20260531...HEAD`
- `git diff origin/runtime-tool-schema-quarantine-20260531...HEAD | rg -i '^\\+.*(dofbot|doftbot)' || true`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall `patch is correct (0.87)`
- AWS Crabbox fresh PR checkout: run `run_2cbf59ca8978`, lease `cbx_fa935ef9ff4c`, slug `tidal-crab`, provider `aws`, type `c7a.8xlarge`; command installed Node 22.19.0 + pnpm 11.2.2, then ran `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`; passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` now reports active runtime tool loading and schema-normalization failures that could otherwise hide fatal assistant-startup tool schema problems.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux fresh PR checkout for openclaw/openclaw#88760 on `c7a.8xlarge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: installed Node 22.19.0 and pnpm 11.2.2 on the fresh PR checkout, ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, then ran `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: `run_2cbf59ca8978` completed with exit code 0; changed gate covered the stacked runtime schema files plus this doctor runtime schema follow-up.

Observed result after fix: doctor runtime schema collection returns actionable findings instead of throwing or silently dropping the bad tool path; focused tests cover construction failure, normalization failure, unreadable schema, bundled MCP normalization failure, and existing runtime schema doctor behavior.

What was not tested: full repo test suite. The remote changed gate was run on the stacked PR checkout because #88735 is the base for this follow-up.
target.author: vincentkoc
